status.message: Latest public summary from the Boscastle flash flood observatory.

summary_metrics.label: Latest depth
summary_metrics.note: Latest published water depth at the observatory reporting point.

summary_metrics.label: Rainfall, last 24h
summary_metrics.note: Total rainfall accumulated across the latest 24-hour reporting window.

summary_metrics.label: Depth change, last 24h
summary_metrics.note: Difference between the latest depth and the published depth 24 hours earlier.

summary_metrics.label: Window maximum
summary_metrics.note: Highest published water depth across the current reporting window.

rainfall.title: Rainfall Over The Last 24 Hours
rainfall.description: Public rainfall totals across the latest reporting window, showing the build-up to the most recent channel response.
rainfall.empty_message: Rainfall data will appear here once the first public payload is published.

depth.title: Observed Water Depth
depth.description: Public water-depth series for the same period, showing how quickly the channel reacts during unsettled conditions.
depth.empty_message: Depth data will appear here once the first public payload is published.

notes.label: Why Boscastle
notes.text: Boscastle is a flashy catchment. Steep slopes and slow-draining geology can produce rapid river response during short, localised rainfall bursts.

notes.label: How it is observed
notes.text: The observatory combines LiDAR, an ultrasonic altimeter, and camera observations to capture how rainfall translates into channel response.

notes.label: Why it matters
notes.text: The aim is to improve flood understanding, early warning, and mitigation strategies for communities exposed to fast-rising flood events.

footer.title: Observatory partners
footer.text: Researchers from the University of Greenwich, the University of Bath, and TU Delft, supported by the Royal Society.
